reply_content:
​@Airlink_SG  first of all, thank you for those long answers, it means you took time to answer me and didn't just write "PSG better".

So, the ball possession for me isn't that big of a factor, it means that PSG is good at making the ball move without the other team getting it...but if you don't convert that possession in goals, and the other team does with much less of it, then it doesn't really matter.
Same thing happened with Inter-Barca, Barca had more possession, but both team scored a lot of goals and Inter got the final blow to get them to the final. In terms of possession and shots taken, Barca got unlucky you would say in your theory, but in reality Inter just defender absolutely better and used their occasions more wisely.

In my opinion psG showed it's full potential in the final, destroying Inter 5-0, but the reason I say it got there by luck is justified by the stuff I said above. With ALL those statistic in their favour, PSG honestly deserved an elimination for not converting all those plays into goals, and getting TO PENALTIES after dominating the game with Liverpool.

Inter on the other hand had the opposite outcome, statistic in their favour (like being down only for 15 minutes up until the final) but still lost since PSG played a perfect game and converted almost ALL of their plays into goals...like they should have done in the whole tournament
